headline: China's control over indium phosphide exports threatens AI data centre rollout
summary: Barely a week after Nvidia-backed chipmaker Coherent warned of a shortage of indium phosphide in an earnings call in early May, its CEO Jim Anderson was on a plane with a U.S. business delegation accompanying President Donald Trump on his trip to China.  Anderson's visit was partly to raise the issue of delays in China's export licenses involving the highly strategic ‌material, essential in manufacturing high-speed optical chips for AI data centres, said three sources familiar with the matter.  The issue was also discussed during talks in Seoul between top trade negotiators of the two countries ahead of Trump's ‌May 14-15 summit with China's President Xi Jinping, according to two U.S. government officials and a person briefed on the talks.

The chip industry has ⁠been grappling with a capacity crunch, as TSMC, the world's largest contract chipmaker, works ⁠to keep up with surging demand and avoid becoming a bottleneck in the global supply chain amid the AI ​boom.

headline: SpaceX's market meteoric debut by the numbers
summary:
STORY: SpaceX's market debut on Friday is expected to be the largest-ever IPO.

Here are the meteoric numbers that have sent the spaceflight business into orbit.

:: $1.75 trillion 

$1.75 trillion is the valuation Elon Musk's company is targeting.

That would instantly rank it among the world's most valuable companies.

:: $135

At $135 per share, SpaceX would trade at a trailing price-to-sales multiple of roughly 94.

That's above companies such as Nvidia, Amazon and Meta, and sees investors asked to pay a premium.

:: 18.67 billion

$18.67 billion is how high company sales reached last year - rising by a third.

:: 10.3 mln

:: 9,600

Starlink network did the heavy lifting making up 60% of the total sales.

That was thanks to its 10.3 million users across 9,600 satellites.

:: 2

After a single launch in 2006, SpaceX has far outpaced rivals, now managing two launches every week.

That makes it the go-to launch partner for NASA and the Pentagon.

But AI losses have weighed.

:: $4.94 billion

Merging with the money-losing xAI pushed the company to a net loss of $4.94 billion last year.

Down from a profit of $791 million in 2024
